attacking_style: Early-race opportunist who attacks at flag drop or infiltrates opening breakaways, prioritizing bonus second accumulation over selective late-race moves. Targets intermediate sprints and stage-long breaks rather than decisive climbing attacks.
team_tactics: Operates independently in breakaway hunting role with minimal visible team setup. Relies on personal positioning and timing rather than domestique-led launches or tempo control.
terrain_preference: Thrives in stage race formats where repeated breakaway opportunities and intermediate sprints allow mathematical accumulation of points and bonuses. Less effective in one-day decisive scenarios.
vulnerability: Overeager pacing leads to premature energy expenditure — cramped catastrophically after immediate attack at UAE Tour. Aggressive early positioning compromises finishing capacity in sprint scenarios.
key_patterns: Infiltrates early breakaways across multiple stages within single stage races (3 observations)
Prioritizes mathematical accumulation strategy over selective aggression for classifications (2 observations)
Targets bonus seconds via intermediate sprints rather than GC-focused efforts (2 observations)
Attacks immediately at race start/flag drop showing high early aggression (2 observations)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2020 showed both strategic maturity (points jersey accumulation tactics) and execution flaws (cramping from overeager pacing), suggesting inconsistent race management despite sound strategic thinking
